title: Next-generation node LINPACK scaling projection
difficulty_justification: Requires retrieving current node benchmark data, applying hardware scaling ratios (core count, memory bandwidth) for the target CPU, projecting cluster-level LINPACK improvement, and noting scaling limitations — multi-step quantitative modeling requiring domain knowledge.
query_text: Based on current performance benchmarks in the environment, model the expected LINPACK improvement if we replace the current CPU nodes with AMD EPYC 9654 nodes (96 cores, 384 GB/s memory bandwidth). Show your calculation.

eval_criteria.gold_answer: Designer should use current benchmark data to estimate single-node LINPACK efficiency, then scale by the ratio of memory bandwidth and core count of the new vs old hardware. AMD EPYC 9654: 96 cores vs current 64 cores (1.5x core ratio), 384 vs current bandwidth. Valid answer shows: (1) current node LINPACK estimate; (2) expected scaling factor from HW specs; (3) total cluster improvement projection; (4) notes on diminishing returns at scale (memory bandwidth, interconnect).

checkpoints.description: Agent queries existing benchmark or performance telemetry data.
Agent computes a scaling factor or percentage improvement estimate.
checkpoints.binary_pass_condition: final_answer contains scaling factor or percentage improvement

checkpoints.description: Agent mentions at least one scaling limitation or caveat.
checkpoints.binary_pass_condition: final_answer mentions at least one scaling limitation
